held that Persons with disabilities can effectively discharge 
their duties if reasonable accommodations are made for them. 
The said judgment further emphasized the State’s obligation 
to ensure their full and effective participation in society. Thus, 
it was argued that the High Court erred in holding that the 
appellant’s disability had no impact on the interview and that 
he was not entitled to any relaxation.

23.3. It was further submitted that the minimum qualifying marks 
should not be imposed in viva voce examination for entry 
level selection, such as, the post of Civil Judge. In support 
of the same, he placed reliance on the decisions of this 
Court in (i) Dr.Kavita Khamboj v. High Court of Punjab and 
Haryana and others,13 in which it was stated that ‘viva voce 
examination may not apply for entry-level junior officers’ and 
(ii)Abhimeet Sinha and Others v. High Court of Judicature at
